title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Install required packages for DiT inference and evaluation. Use pre-built DiT codebase and evaluation tools.

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.1+ with CUDA 12.1: pip install torch torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install diffusers library: pip install diffusers==0.29.0 transformers accelerate
steps.step4: Install evaluation dependencies: pip install pytorch-fid lpips clip-by-openai clean-fid
steps.step5: Install utility packages: pip install numpy scipy matplotlib seaborn tqdm einops
steps.step6: Clone DiT reference implementation and verify GPU availability with nvidia-smi

title: Prepare ImageNet Validation Subset for Evaluation
description: Download and prepare ImageNet validation data and class-conditional labels for DiT-XL/2 evaluation. Use 10K samples for FID computation.

steps.step1: Download ImageNet 256x256 validation images (~1.3GB) from HuggingFace: huggingface-cli download --repo-type dataset imagenet-1k val --local-dir ./data/imagenet/val
steps.step2: Download class labels file ILSVRC2012_validation_ground_truth.txt and map to class indices
steps.step3: Create 10K balanced subset (100 images per class) with random seed 42 for reproducibility
steps.step4: Precompute ground truth Inception features for FID: python -m pytorch_fid --save-stats ./data/imagenet/val_gt.npz ./data/imagenet/val
steps.step5: Generate class-conditional prompts (class indices 0-999) for DiT-XL/2 class-conditional generation
steps.step6: Verify data integrity: count images (expected: 10000), check image dimensions (256x256)

title: Baseline: Full DiT-XL/2 Inference (50 steps)
description: Establish baseline performance with full DiT-XL/2 model (28 blocks, 50 DDIM steps). This is the reference for all comparisons.

steps.step1: Load pre-trained DiT-XL/2-256x256 model from HuggingFace: facebook/DiT-XL-2-256
steps.step2: Generate 10K images with DDIM scheduler (50 steps, guidance_scale=1.0 for class-conditional)
steps.step3: Use random seeds [42, 123, 2024] for 3 independent runs
steps.step4: Compute FID, IS using pytorch-fid and custom IS implementation
steps.step5: Measure per-step FLOPs using fvcore or manual counting (28 blocks * per-block FLOPs)
steps.step6: Measure wall-clock time per image (batch_size=1) and throughput (batch_size=4)
steps.step7: Store results: FID_mean, FID_std, IS_mean, total_FLOPs, time_per_image

title: Baseline: DeepCache (Training-Free Caching)
description: Implement DeepCache baseline - caches high-level features across timesteps. Represents training-free caching approaches.

steps.step1: Implement DeepCache for DiT: cache intermediate features every N steps (N=5 for 50-step generation)
steps.step2: Configure cache strategy: cache blocks 20-28 (high-level features), refresh blocks 1-20
steps.step3: Generate 10K images with same random seeds [42, 123, 2024]
steps.step4: Compute FID, IS and compare to full model
steps.step5: Measure speedup ratio (expected ~1.5-2x), actual FLOPs reduction
steps.step6: Record cache hit rate and analyze quality degradation vs speedup tradeoff

title: Baseline: Δ-DiT (Fixed Schedule Layer Skipping)
description: Implement Δ-DiT baseline - uses fixed schedules to skip front/back blocks based on timestep. Represents training-free layer skipping.

steps.step1: Implement Δ-DiT stage-adaptive caching: skip rear blocks (22-28) for t > 25, skip front blocks (1-7) for t <= 25
steps.step2: Use Δ-Cache mechanism: cache feature offsets rather than full features
steps.step3: Configure skip intervals to achieve ~30% FLOP reduction matching CAD-DiT target
steps.step5: Compute FID, IS and FLOPs reduction
steps.step6: This baseline applies UNIFORM depth reduction (same for all tokens) - key comparison point for spatial adaptation

title: Baseline: Global Early Exit (No Spatial Adaptation)
description: Implement global early exit baseline - all tokens exit at same layer based on timestep. Shows importance of per-token adaptation.

steps.step1: Implement global threshold: use average consistency across all tokens to determine exit layer
steps.step2: Use same τ_base and α parameters as CAD-DiT main method
steps.step3: All tokens in a layer exit together when average PCM > threshold
steps.step6: This ablation isolates the benefit of spatially-varying (per-token) vs global depth allocation

title: CAD-DiT: Consistency-Adaptive Depth with Per-Token Exit
description: Main proposed method. Implements Prediction Consistency Metric (PCM) for per-token adaptive depth without training.

steps.step1: Implement PCM computation: PCM_i^l = 1 - ||ε_i^l - ε_i^(l-1)|| / (||ε_i^(l-1)|| + δ), with δ=1e-6
steps.step2: Implement token-conditional forward pass: track active_set of tokens, compute PCM after each layer
steps.step3: Implement timestep-aware threshold: τ(t) = τ_base * (1 - α * t/T), default τ_base=0.95, α=0.3
steps.step4: For each token: if PCM_i^l > τ(t), freeze prediction and exclude from subsequent layers
steps.step6: For best config, run 3 seeds [42, 123, 2024] for 10K images each
steps.step7: Compute FID, IS, per-step exit statistics (mean exit layer per timestep), spatial exit heatmaps

title: Ablation: Effect of Consistency Threshold τ_base
description: Ablation study on base consistency threshold. Higher threshold = more conservative = later exit.

steps.step2: For each value, generate 2K images (seed=42 only) for efficiency
steps.step3: Measure: FID, average FLOPs reduction, percentage of tokens exiting before layer 28
steps.step4: Plot FID vs FLOPs reduction curve showing quality-efficiency tradeoff
steps.step6: Expected: τ_base=0.95 provides ~35% FLOP reduction with <3 FID increase

title: Ablation: Effect of Timestep Modulation α
description: Ablation on timestep-aware thresholding. Tests whether dynamic thresholds help vs static.

steps.step3: Measure FID, FLOPs reduction, exit layer distribution across timesteps
steps.step4: Analyze: α=0 should be worse (no timestep adaptation), α=0.3 expected optimal
steps.step5: Plot exit layer vs timestep curves for different α values
steps.step6: Compare with oracle: uniform exit depth vs adaptive

title: Ablation: Consistency Metric Variants
description: Test different consistency metrics to validate PCM design choice.

steps.step1: Variant 1: Cosine similarity between consecutive predictions (instead of normalized L2)
steps.step3: Variant 3: Momentum-based consistency (EMA of recent predictions)
steps.step4: Variant 4: Layer-normalized consistency (normalize across tokens in layer)
steps.step5: For each variant, generate 2K images with default thresholds
steps.step6: Compare FID and FLOPs reduction to validate PCM design
steps.step7: Document which variant provides best quality-efficiency tradeoff

title: Spatial Pattern Analysis
description: Analyze which image regions exit early vs late to validate that consistency correlates with semantic complexity.

steps.step1: For 500 generated images, record exit layer for each spatial token (16x16=256 tokens for DiT-XL/2 256x256)
steps.step2: Compute average exit layer per spatial position across dataset
steps.step3: Generate heatmap visualization: red = late exit (complex regions), blue = early exit (simple regions)
steps.step4: Correlate with semantic segmentation: load pre-trained segmentation model (Mask2Former)
steps.step5: Quantify: average exit depth for background vs foreground vs object boundaries
steps.step6: Hypothesis: background tokens exit 5-10 layers earlier than foreground object tokens

title: Timestep Pattern Analysis
description: Analyze how exit patterns vary across denoising timesteps.

steps.step1: Record exit statistics per timestep (0-49 for 50-step DDIM)
steps.step2: Compute: average exit layer, percentage of early exits, FLOPs per timestep
steps.step3: Plot curves: exit layer vs timestep, cumulative FLOPs vs timestep
steps.step4: Identify patterns: early timesteps should show more aggressive exiting
steps.step5: Compare against theoretical optimal: uniform FLOPs distribution vs adaptive
steps.step6: Quantify timestep efficiency: FLOPs saved in early vs late denoising

title: Robustness Evaluation
description: Test CAD-DiT robustness across different generation settings.

steps.step1: Vary number of sampling steps: 20, 30, 50 (default), 100 steps
steps.step2: For each step count, use best CAD-DiT config (τ_base=0.95, α=0.3)
steps.step3: Generate 2K images per setting, compute FID
steps.step5: Verify method works across different schedulers: DDIM, DPM++ 2M, Euler
steps.step6: Document which settings CAD-DiT is most/least effective

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform rigorous statistical tests to validate improvements.

steps.step1: Run 5 independent trials with different seeds for CAD-DiT, Δ-DiT, and Full baseline
steps.step2: For each trial, compute FID on 10K images
steps.step3: Perform paired t-test: CAD-DiT vs Δ-DiT at same FLOP budget
steps.step4: Perform paired t-test: CAD-DiT vs Global Early Exit (ablation)
steps.step5: Report p-values and confidence intervals for FID differences
steps.step6: Verify success criteria: p < 0.05 for quality improvement at same/reduced compute

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Verify all success criteria from proposal are met or document failure.

steps.step1: Criterion 1: ≥30% per-step FLOP reduction with FID <3 points increase. Measure and report.
steps.step2: Criterion 2: Spatial patterns show variation. Report std dev of exit depths across spatial positions.
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Works without retraining. Verify: no model weights modified, no gradients computed.
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Qualitative visual quality. Conduct side-by-side comparison of 100 sample pairs.
steps.step5: If any criterion fails, document failure mode and analyze root cause
steps.step6: Generate final verdict: CONFIRMED, PARTIALLY CONFIRMED, or REFUTED

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create publication-quality figures for the research paper.

steps.step1: Figure 1: Method overview diagram - show token-conditional forward pass with PCM computation
steps.step2: Figure 2: Spatial exit heatmaps for 8 example images - show original image + exit depth overlay
steps.step3: Figure 3: FID vs FLOPs tradeoff curve - compare CAD-DiT, baselines, and ablations
steps.step4: Figure 4: Exit pattern analysis - (a) exit layer vs timestep, (b) exit distribution, (c) FLOPs per timestep
steps.step5: Figure 5: Qualitative comparison - 4x4 grid showing original, full model, Δ-DiT, CAD-DiT outputs
steps.step6: Figure 6: Ablation study results - bar charts for τ_base and α sweeps
steps.step7: Table 1: Main results - FID, IS, FLOPs reduction for all methods with standard deviations
steps.step8: Save all figures as high-resolution PNG and vector PDF formats

steps.step1: Supp Figure 1: More qualitative examples (20 images across different classes)
steps.step2: Supp Figure 2: Failure case analysis - images where CAD-DiT performs poorly
steps.step3: Supp Figure 3: Per-class FID comparison - performance across ImageNet classes
steps.step4: Supp Table 1: Full hyperparameter search results
steps.step5: Supp Table 2: Computational overhead analysis - PCM computation cost
steps.step6: Document experimental protocol: exact commands, random seeds, software versions
steps.step7: Create reproducibility checklist with all configuration details
